question: I heard you can claim both 80CCD(1B) and 80CCD(2) together for a total NPS deduction of ₹1 lakh. Is that right?
ground_truth: No. 80CCD(1B) allows an additional ₹50,000 deduction beyond 80C. 80CCD(2) covers employer NPS contributions and has no fixed cap (up to 14% of salary for central government). They can be claimed together but the combined deduction is not capped at ₹1 lakh.
evaluation_notes: Tests whether model can handle a partially-correct premise mixed with a wrong conclusion.

question: What is the HRA exemption if you live in a non-metro city and pay rent of ₹20000 per month with a basic salary of ₹40000?
ground_truth: The HRA exemption is the minimum of: (1) actual HRA received (2) 40% of basic salary = ₹16000/month for non-metro (3) rent paid minus 10% of basic = ₹20000 - ₹4000 = ₹16000. So exemption is ₹16000 per month.
expected_citations: HRA exemption = minimum of actual HRA received, 40% of basic salary (non-metro), or rent paid minus 10% of basic

question: Is it true that you CANNOT claim a home loan interest deduction under Section 24b if the property is self-occupied?
ground_truth: False. Under Section 24b, you CAN claim a deduction of up to ₹2 lakh per year on home loan interest for a self-occupied property.
expected_citations: interest deduction up to ₹2 lakh per annum for a self-occupied property
evaluation_notes: Tests if the model correctly rejects a negation trap — the premise is false.

question: Can I claim Section 80D deduction for health insurance of my adult son who earns his own income?
ground_truth: No. Section 80D deductions can be claimed for health insurance premiums paid for self, spouse, and dependent children. An adult child who earns their own income is generally not considered dependent, so the premium may not qualify.
expected_citations: premiums paid for self spouse and dependent children
evaluation_notes: Tests eligibility boundary — dependent vs non-dependent adult child.

question: What is TDS rate for rent payments above ₹50000 per month?
ground_truth: Under Section 194-I, TDS on rent of plant/machinery is 2% and on land/building/furniture is 10%. The threshold for TDS on rent is ₹2,40,000 per year (not ₹50,000 per month).
evaluation_notes: Tests specific threshold knowledge and correct section citation.

question: Is LTCG on equity mutual funds fully exempt from tax?
ground_truth: No. Long-term capital gains (LTCG) on equity mutual funds exceeding ₹1 lakh in a financial year are taxable at 10% under Section 112A, without the benefit of indexation.
expected_citations: LTCG on equity above ₹1 lakh taxed at 10% under Section 112A
evaluation_notes: Common misconception — LTCG was exempt pre-2018 but Section 112A removed the exemption above ₹1L.

question: I contribute ₹2 lakh per year to PPF. How much of that is tax-deductible under 80C?
ground_truth: Only ₹1.5 lakh is deductible under Section 80C, since that is the maximum aggregate deduction limit under 80C. The remaining ₹50,000 above ₹1.5 lakh is not deductible.
expected_citations: The aggregate deduction under Section 80C is limited to ₹1
column13: Tests whether model correctly applies the cap even when contribution exceeds it.

question: My employer contributes 15% of my basic salary to NPS. Is the full employer contribution deductible under 80CCD(2)?
ground_truth: Under Section 80CCD(2), the employer NPS contribution is deductible up to 14% of basic salary for central government employees and 10% for private sector employees. If your employer contributes 15%, only up to the applicable limit (10% or 14%) is deductible.
expected_citations: employer contribution deductible up to 14% of salary
evaluation_notes: Tests cap application when contribution exceeds the deductible limit.

question: Can I claim both Section 80C and Section 80D deductions in the same year?
ground_truth: Yes. Section 80C and Section 80D are separate deduction provisions. You can claim up to ₹1.5 lakh under 80C and up to ₹25,000 (or ₹50,000 for senior citizens) under 80D in the same financial year.
evaluation_notes: Tests multi-section knowledge combination — both deductions are available simultaneously.

question: Under the new tax regime (2024) can I still claim 80C deductions?
ground_truth: No. The new tax regime introduced in Budget 2020 (and made default from FY 2023-24) does not allow deductions under Chapter VI-A including Section 80C. These deductions are only available under the old tax regime.
expected_citations: new tax regime does not allow most deductions
evaluation_notes: Tests regime-specific knowledge — common confusion point.

question: What happens if TDS is not deducted by the payer?
ground_truth: If the payer fails to deduct TDS, they may be liable for interest under Section 201(1A) at 1% per month from the date TDS was deductible to the date it was actually deducted, and 1.5% per month from deduction date to deposit date.
evaluation_notes: Tests knowledge of consequences — important edge case.

question: Is HRA exemption available for employees working from home who pay rent to a family member?
ground_truth: HRA exemption can be claimed if you pay rent to a family member (including parents) provided the rent arrangement is genuine, rental agreement exists, and rent income is declared in the landlord's (family member's) return. However, you cannot claim HRA if you pay rent to your spouse.
expected_citations: rent paid to family member including parents
evaluation_notes: Edge case: allowed for parents but not spouse — nuanced eligibility.
